title: hermione italica var. princeps
intro: Hermione italica var. princeps, also known as wild bergamot or bergamot mint, is a perennial plant belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae) and is native to Europe and Asia. The plant's leaves and flowers have been traditionally used for their medicinal properties, which include antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral effects. The health benefits of Hermione italica var. princeps include reducing anxiety and stress, improving digestion, and relieving symptoms of colds and flu. Herbal preparations of the plant can be consumed as teas, infusions, or tinctures, and can also be used in aromatherapy and as a natural insect repellent.
